title: Freshly Fried British Peas Cornish Pasty
description: A savory British dish featuring frozen peas in a double-fried crust, served in a traditional Cornish pasty form, perfect for a cozy meal. This satvik-friendly recipe is a twist on the classic KFC-style double fry method.
instructions: Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the flour, salt, and pepper.
Add the cold butter to the flour mixture and use a pastry blender to work it into a crumbly mixture.
Gradually add the water to the flour mixture, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together.
Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for 5 minutes.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each piece into a thin circle.
Place 1/4 cup of thawed peas in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the peas to form a triangle or circle, and press the edges together to seal.
Double fry the pasties by frying them at 325 degrees Fahrenheit for 5 minutes, then increasing the heat to 375 degrees Fahrenheit and frying for an additional 2 minutes.
Drain the pasties on paper towels and serve hot with a squeeze of lemon juice.
